selection_rationale: Two HAL Generalist Agent runs on GAIA validation: o4-mini High and Claude 3.7 Sonnet. Same agent harness, two frontier models. Provides a within-harness model-vs-model contrast for cross-benchmark cross-checks against the TAU-bench Tool Calling fixture. GAIA's HAL artifacts only have HAL Generalist and HF Open Deep Research harnesses, no TAU-style Tool Calling — so cross-benchmark, the scaffold differs (Tool Calling vs HAL Generalist), but within GAIA the comparison is harness-internal.
transformations: decrypted with Fernet/PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 using public anti-scrape value (redacted)
extracted per-task rows from raw_eval_results (score:bool, explanation:str)
aggregated per-task tokens by summing summary.usage[model] over raw_logging_results grouped by weave_task_id
preserved per-model token breakdown as tokens_in_by_model / tokens_out_by_model (JSON-encoded)
inlined results.{accuracy, level_1_accuracy, level_2_accuracy, level_3_accuracy} as run_level_accuracies (per-row repeat)
added derived columns: success_bool (bool score), outcome_status ('graded' | 'errored')
redaction_policy: Same as tau-bench: do not republish raw_logging_results contents. The grader_explanation field is preserved (it is grader meta-text describing the parsed answer, not the model's answer text), but truncated to 500 chars as a precaution.

leaderboard_cross_check.note: GAIA leaderboard at hal.cs.princeton.edu/gaia not consulted in this scouting pass; sample matches official_accuracy in trace.
Sample matches official_accuracy in trace (0.564). Cost reconciliation in task 5.4 should compare against any leaderboard-displayed cost.

candidate_claim.text: On GAIA validation, HAL Generalist with o4-mini High (54.5%) and HAL Generalist with Claude 3.7 Sonnet (56.4%) differ by 1.9 percentage points; Claude is 2.2x more expensive ($130.68 vs $59.39). The reanalysis asks whether the 1.9 pp accuracy advantage is statistically distinguishable from noise on n=165, and whether it is decision-relevant given the cost gap.
candidate_claim.rationale: Within-harness model comparison with a narrow accuracy gap and a wide cost gap. n=165 is larger than TAU's n=50, so power is better, but Holm-Bonferroni across the full HAL Generalist family will tighten the bar.

scouting_lessons.level_metadata_lost_in_translation: GAIA's source dataset records each task's difficulty Level 1/2/3, but HAL's raw_eval_results drops this metadata — only aggregate level_*_accuracy survives. Stratified per-level analysis on HAL traces requires re-joining the gated gaia-benchmark/GAIA dataset by task_id. The toolkit should make this join first-class rather than hidden.
